title: Calamagrostis aurea
intro: Calamagrostis aurea, also known as golden feather grass, is a perennial grass native to North America, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Calamagrostis aurea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calamagrostis aurea is often prepared as a tea or decoction, or used in combination with other herbs in tinctures and infusions to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
taxonomy: Calamagrostis aurea is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Calamagrostis and is native to North America. The genus Calamagrostis is further classified within the tribe Poeae. Calamagrostis aurea is also known as golden rice grass due to its yellow or golden color. Its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Angiospermophyta, Class: Liliopsida, Order: Poales, Family: Poaceae, Genus: Calamagrostis, Species: C. aurea.
